title: carlina corymbosa subsp. graeca
intro: Carlina corymbosa subsp. graeca, also known as Greek Carline Thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. The health benefits of Carlina corymbosa subsp. graeca include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and menstrual problems.
